Use when setting up authentication, authorization, login flows, access tokens, API security, or securing Fastify routes with OAuth; also applies when troubleshooting token validation errors, mismatched redirect URIs, CSRF issues, scope problems, or RFC 6749/6750/7636/8252/8628 compliance questions.

children.name: node-backend
children.description: Use when working with Node.js backends using Express, NestJS, Fastify, REST/GraphQL APIs, controllers, services, validation, logging, security, testing, and backend architecture.

children.name: node-inspect-debugger
children.description: Debug Node.js via --inspect + Chrome DevTools Protocol CLI.

children.name: openapi-api-designer
children.description: Use when planning, designing, drafting, or refactoring HTTP REST API contracts as OpenAPI 3.2 documents, especially when business capabilities, resource boundaries, or AI-agent consumption quality matter.

children.name: plan
children.description: Plan mode: write markdown plan to .hermes/plans/, no exec.

children.name: python-cli-patterns
children.description: Use when designing, building, refactoring, or reviewing Python command-line tools so they are ergonomic, composable, testable, and production-friendly.

children.name: python-debugpy
children.description: Debug Python: pdb REPL + debugpy remote (DAP).

children.name: python-observability
children.description: Python observability patterns including structured logging, metrics, and distributed tracing. Use when adding logging, implementing metrics collection, setting up tracing, or debugging production systems.

children.name: python-performance-optimization
children.description: Profile and optimize Python code using cProfile, memory profilers, and performance best practices. Use when debugging slow Python code, optimizing bottlenecks, or improving application performance.

children.name: python-type-safety
children.description: Python type safety with type hints, generics, protocols, and strict type checking. Use when adding type annotations, implementing generic classes, defining structural interfaces, or configuring mypy/pyright.

children.name: python-dev
children.description: Use when building or modifying Python projects, services, Lambda handlers, or CLIs with Hermes-native repo inspection, uv-based workflows, typing, testing, and deployment hygiene.

children.name: requesting-code-review
children.description: Pre-commit review: security scan, quality gates, auto-fix.

children.name: spike
children.description: Throwaway experiments to validate an idea before build.

children.name: spring-boot-engineer
children.description: Use when building, reviewing, or evolving Spring Boot services, REST APIs, reactive endpoints, data access layers, security, and cloud-native service integrations. Covers practical defaults for modern Spring Boot applications with strong testing, observability, and maintainable architecture.

children.name: subagent-driven-development
children.description: Execute plans via delegate_task subagents (2-stage review).

children.name: systematic-debugging
children.description: 4-phase root cause debugging: understand bugs before fixing.

children.name: test-driven-development
children.description: TDD: enforce RED-GREEN-REFACTOR, tests before code.

children.name: writing-plans
children.description: Write implementation plans: bite-sized tasks, paths, code.
